title: Myrcia neocollina
taxonomy: Myrcia neocollina is classified within the family Myrtaceae, which comprises a diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the myrtle family. This family is further divided into several genera, with Myrcia being one of the largest and most widespread genera within Myrtaceae. Myrcia neocollina is a species within the Myrcia genus, which is native to the tropical regions of the Americas. The Myrtaceae family is part of the order Myrtales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Penaeaceae and Oliniaceae. Myrcia neocollina's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Myrtaceae family.

intro: Myrcia neocollina, also known as the Brazilian myrtle, is a small tree or shrub native to the tropical regions of Brazil. Its leaves have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, offering potential health benefits in the treatment of various conditions, including fever, colds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Myrcia neocollina include flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Myrcia neocollina is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, or as a extract for use in capsules or tinctures, allowing for its bioactive compounds to be effectively utilized.
